title: 1905 Lunaris Comets season
content:
Greta Aldridge (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as point guard of the Lunaris Comets when they won the Lakefront Championship Series in 1905.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Lakefront Championship Series, Greta Aldridge remained with the Lunaris Comets for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Championship season.

The 1905 Lakefront Championship Series campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Greta Aldridge averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Harrowgate Memorial Arena in Harrowgate.

Professional career.

Greta Aldridge joined the Lunaris Comets and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1900s. By 1904, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Awards and honors.

Following the Lakefront Championship Series, Greta Aldridge received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Legacy.

Historians of the Lunaris Comets cite Greta Aldridge's 1905 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Harrowgate Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Personal life.

Greta Aldridge retired to Harrowgate and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1907.

Early life and college career.

Greta Aldridge played college basketball in Harrowgate before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as point guard.
